## Quick orientation

This repo is a Lean 4 / mathlib-derived project that builds a large theorem library (under `Lemma/`) and a companion website (PHP + static assets) that visualizes Lean code with KaTeX. Key tools: `lean` / `elan`, `lake` (build tool), and standard shell/PowerShell scripts under `sh/` and `ps1/`.

Keep changes small and local: many files are interdependent (imports are computed from file paths). Use the project's scripts to recompute imports and update downstream artifacts.

## Essential commands
- Build the Lean project (Linux/macOS):
  - `lake build`
- Recompute import/test SQL and push lemma metadata into MySQL (requires env vars `MYSQL_USER` and `MYSQL_PWD`):
  - Linux: `bash sh/run.sh`
  - Windows PowerShell: `.\ps1\run.ps1`
- Update pinned package dependencies (mathlib and other packages):
  - `bash sh/update.sh` or `.\ps1\update.ps1`
- Quick local check used by CI locally:
  - `lake setup-file test.lean` (used by `sh/run.sh` to compute modules/imports)

## Files and directories you will use most
- `Lemma/` ÔÇö the Lean library. Most PRs change files here. Many subdirs (e.g. `Lemma/Bool/`, `Lemma/Int/`, `Lemma/Tensor/`).
- `*.lean` vs `*.echo.lean` ÔÇö the scripts and `sh/run.sh` intentionally skip `*.echo.lean` files. Prefer editing plain `.lean` files.
- `lake-manifest.json`, `lakefile.toml`, `.lake/` ÔÇö lake packages are pinned here (mathlib, aesop, Qq, etc.). Use `lake` commands to change/update.
- `json/` ÔÇö precomputed indices like `mathlib.jsonl`, `mathlib.tsv`, and other JSON used by the website. If you change export formats, update these.
- `php/`, `website/`, and `static/` ÔÇö web server code that renders search pages and KaTeX visualizations. `index.php`, `query.php`, and `Lemma/` static rendering interact with Lean-sourced data.
- `sh/` and `ps1/` ÔÇö repository maintenance scripts (`run.sh`, `update.sh`, `utility.sh`, and Windows `.ps1` variants). Read them before automating changes.

## Naming and code conventions (explicit project rules)
- CamelCase is used for unary functions/lemmas (examples: `LogSumExp` in `README.md`).
- Snake_Case is used for binary functions: `Eq_Log`, `DivOneTwo` avoided by apostrophe rules.
- Apostrophe separates consecutive digits: `Div1'2` means `1 / 2`.
- Infix operators map to capitalized predicates (examples in `README.md`): `X.eq.Y` Ôćĺ `EqXY`; `X.gt.Y` Ôćĺ `GtXY`.
- Plural `S` and Identity abbreviations are used heavily (see `README.md` section ÔÇťLemma Naming ConventionÔÇŁ). Follow existing names when adding new lemmas.

## Project-specific patterns you must respect
- Many build / metadata scripts parse attributes in Lean source (e.g. `@[main, comm]`, `mp`, `mpr`, `mp.comm`, `comm.is`, and `mt` tokens). See `sh/run.sh` for examples of how these attributes are interpreted and transformed into alternative module names. If you modify attribute usage, update `sh/run.sh` logic accordingly.
- Import map is generated by `lake setup-file test.lean` then processed by `sh/run.sh`. Do not hand-edit `test.lean` or `test.sql` unless you fully understand the pipeline.
- Avoid mass renames of modules without updating import paths ÔÇö `sh/run.sh` and `lake` build rely on module Ôćö file path mapping (module = path under `Lemma/` with `.` separators). Use `lake` tooling and `git mv` for renames.

## Integration points & external deps
- mathlib4 and other third-party lean packages are pinned in `lake-manifest.json` (do not change pinned revisions without coordinating updates). Use `sh/update.sh` to incrementally update dependencies.
- The `sh/run.sh` script writes SQL to `test.sql` and requires a running MySQL server (database `axiom`) if you want to populate the site's lemma table. Required env vars: `MYSQL_USER`, `MYSQL_PWD` (and optional `MYSQL_PORT`). The script will attempt to create the DB/table if missing.
- Frontend visualization depends on static assets in `static/` and PHP endpoints in `php/`. If you change Lean AST->HTML output, update these components and re-generate any JSON indices in `json/`.

## Minimal checklist for common tasks
- Make a small Lean change: edit `Lemma/.../*.lean` Ôćĺ run `lake build` Ôćĺ run `bash sh/run.sh` (or `.\ps1\run.ps1`) to refresh DB/index Ôćĺ smoke test the web pages in `website/`/`php/`.
- Add a new lemma file: ensure path matches module name (e.g. `Lemma/Tensor/NewLemma.lean` Ôćĺ module `Lemma.Tensor.NewLemma`) and follow naming conventions.

## Quick examples
- Rebuild and push lemma metadata to MySQL (Linux):
  - `bash sh/run.sh` (requires `MYSQL_USER`/`MYSQL_PWD`)
- Recompute imports only:
  - `lake setup-file test.lean`

## If something breaks
- Check `.lake/build` and `test.log` created by `sh/run.sh` and `lake setup-file` for failing module builds or `sorry` warnings.
- If CI pins fail, inspect `lake-manifest.json` and run `sh/update.sh` to update dependencies incrementally.

If any of these areas are unclear or you'd like more examples (renaming modules, attribute handling in `sh/run.sh`, or how the website consumes `json/` artifacts), tell me which area to expand and I'll iterate.
